## Overview

**v0.5.1 — fullscreen on every desktop, plus cross-platform polish.** A
maintenance release on top of the v0.5.0 Linux launch: the Alt+Enter fullscreen
toggle now reaches every Linux desktop, the quit-confirmation dialog behaves
the same on Linux, macOS, and Windows, and macOS gains zero idle CPU and correct
rendering across mixed-DPI monitors.

**macOS now requires macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later** — see Migration below.

No config schema changes since v0.5.0.

## What's new since v0.5.0

### New

- **Fullscreen on every desktop** ([#87](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/87))
  — Alt+Enter (cover the panels) and Shift+Alt+Enter (keep the panels visible)
  now work on Linux too, matching Windows and macOS. layer-shell desktops (KDE
  Plasma, sway, Hyprland, COSMIC) anchor to all four edges; GNOME and Cinnamon
  use `xdg_toplevel.set_fullscreen` / `set_maximized`. The fullscreen state
  survives an F1 hide/show.
- **Perf-snapshot hotkey** ([#160](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/160))
  — Ctrl+Shift+F12 (Shift+Cmd+F12 on macOS) writes a render/read-loop timing
  snapshot to the log for diagnostics. Windows already had it; Linux and macOS
  now match.

### Fixes

- **Consistent quit dialog** ([#250](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/250))
  — the quit-confirmation dialog (Cmd+Q / Alt+F4) now follows one standard on
  every platform: Enter = Quit, Esc = Cancel.
- **Shell-gone notification** ([#248](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/248))
  — if the shell binary disappears at runtime (e.g. Homebrew moves `bash` on an
  update), opening a tab now reports the error instead of dying silently.
- **No input leak while hidden — GNOME / Cinnamon** ([#247](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/247))
  — hiding now hands keyboard focus to another window, so keystrokes (and the
  fullscreen toggle) no longer reach the hidden terminal.
- **GNOME autostart** ([#246](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/246)) — the
  shared XDG autostart entry is now kept with `NotShowIn=GNOME` instead of being
  deleted, fixing autostart breakage after switching between desktops and a
  double launch on the first GNOME login.
- **Mixed-DPI monitors — macOS** ([#253](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/253))
  — moving the window between monitors of different scale now re-measures the
  cell metrics, so glyph size and layout stay correct (matching Windows and
  Linux).
- **Cursor-monitor drop-down — macOS** ([#240](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/240))
  — the drop-down opens on the monitor under the pointer.

### Performance (macOS)

- **Zero idle CPU** ([#255](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/255)) —
  rendering is driven by `CADisplayLink`; drawing suspends while the terminal is
  hidden and frames are skipped while it is visible but idle. Requires macOS 14+.
- Read-loop aligned to the Linux-style poll→read path
  ([#254](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/254)) and cross-platform
  render / read-loop timing instrumentation
  ([#160](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/160)).

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows 10/11 x64 | `tildaz-v0.5.1-win-x64.zip` | Unzip anywhere. `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY. Unsigned (same SmartScreen / AV handling as before). |
| Windows 11 ARM64 | `tildaz-v0.5.1-win-arm64.zip` | ARM64-native binary + ARM64 ConPTY. Unsigned. |
| macOS 14+ universal | `tildaz-v0.5.1-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel. Drag `TildaZ.app` to Applications. Ad-hoc signed. |
| Linux portable (glibc 2.28+) | `tildaz-v0.5.1-linux-{x86_64,aarch64}.tar.gz` | Extract, run `./install.sh` (writes `.desktop` + icon under `~/.local/share/`, no `sudo`). |
| Linux Debian / Ubuntu | `tildaz_0.5.1_{amd64,arm64}.deb` | `sudo dpkg -i tildaz_*.deb` or open with the Software app. |
| Linux Fedora / RHEL / openSUSE | `tildaz-0.5.1-1.{x86_64,aarch64}.rpm` | `sudo dnf install ./tildaz-*.rpm` |
| Linux distro-independent | `TildaZ-0.5.1-{x86_64,aarch64}.AppImage` | `chmod +x` and run. FUSE optional. glibc 2.28+. |
| Arch / Manjaro / EndeavourOS | `tildaz-0.5.1-1-x86_64.pkg.tar.zst` | `sudo pacman -U tildaz-*.pkg.tar.zst` (x86_64 only — Arch's official arch). |

**A running Wayland session (compositor) is required — TildaZ does not run
under X11.** It speaks the Wayland protocol directly over the socket, so it does
not link `libwayland-client`; the only build-time link is `glibc 2.28+`. The
`.deb` / `.rpm` declare `libxkbcommon`, `libfreetype`, `libfontconfig` as hard
dependencies; `libharfbuzz`, `libdbus-1`, and `libgio-2.0`, plus the
`xdg-desktop-portal` service, are recommended and loaded at runtime via `dlopen`,
degrading to a feature-off state (with a clear log line) when absent.

## Migration from v0.5.0

- **macOS minimum is now macOS 14 (Sonoma).** v0.5.1 drives rendering with
  `CADisplayLink` ([#255](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/255)), which is
  macOS 14+. macOS 13 users should stay on v0.5.0.
- **No config schema changes.** Linux and Windows have no migration steps;
  `~/.config/tildaz/config.json` (and the Windows/macOS equivalents) are read
  unchanged.

## Known limitations

- **macOS info / error dialog keys are intermittent** ([#249](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/249))
  — the NSAlert-based info / error dialog can occasionally ignore Esc / Enter
  because the accessory-app modal does not always become the key window.
  Investigation continues; the quit-confirmation dialog ([#250](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/250))
  is unaffected.
- **KDE floating dock overlap** ([#206](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/206))
  — a Plasma *floating* panel can be partially covered; this needs a KWin-side
  defloat that layer-shell clients cannot trigger (KDE Bug 491006). Workaround:
  turn off the panel's *Floating* option. Same root cause affects yakuake.
- **KDE Alt+F4 → Cancel flicker** ([#239](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/239))
  — KWin closes the focused layer surface on Alt+F4, so Cancel must recreate it,
  which is briefly visible. Compositor-inherent; OK-to-quit is unaffected.
- **VSCode / Electron (X11) Hangul IME** ([#194](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/194))
  — an fcitx5 frontend-separation limitation, not fixable at the protocol level.
- The software `wl_shm` renderer is a placeholder until a GPU path lands.
